headline: SILVER ROCKETS 128% YEAR-OVER-YEAR TO $75.73; Gold Holds $4,702 as Iran Stalemate Fuels Precious Metals Surge
snippet: Silver hit $75.73/oz on April 27 — up 128.70% year-over-year and 8.14% in a single month — dramatically outpacing gold's $4,702/oz (up 40% YoY) as the prolonged US-Iran stalemate and Strait of Hormuz disruptions sustain safe-haven and industrial demand. Both metals are being driven by persistent central bank buying and inflation expectations that show no sign of cooling.

headline: USA RARE EARTH Seals $2.8B Serra Verde Deal — Controls the Only Non-Asian Source of All Four Magnetic Rare Earths
snippet: USA Rare Earth (Nasdaq: USAR) closed its acquisition of Serra Verde Group for $300M cash plus 126.8M newly issued shares (~$2.8B total), securing the only rare earth mine outside Asia capable of supplying all four magnetic rare earths at scale. Shares rose 5.4% on more than 10 million in volume, reflecting the strategic significance of the deal as Western governments race to build rare earth supply chains independent of China.

headline: TERBIUM SURGES 20.7% to $970/kg, NdPr Alloy Up 138% Year-to-Date as Rare Earth Supply Crunch Deepens
snippet: Rare earth prices are posting their largest monthly gains in years through April 2026, with terbium surging 20.7% to $970/kg and neodymium-praseodymium alloy up 138% year-to-date as Western governments and manufacturers accelerate efforts to build ex-China supply chains for permanent magnet materials. Analysts project NdPr oxide prices will remain well above $100/kg through Q2, with further upside tied to EV and defence procurement programs.

headline: COPPER HOLDS $6/lb AS GOLDMAN MAINTAINS BULL CASE; Traxys Targets $15,000/t as China Sulfuric Acid Ban Looms May 1
snippet: Copper held at $6.03/lb ($13,300/tonne) on April 27, up 10% over the past month, as Goldman Sachs maintained its $12,650/t average forecast while Traxys CEO Mark Kristoff projected $15,000/t within 24–36 months. China's May 1 ban on sulfuric acid exports — critical to roughly 15% of global refined copper output — combines with the ongoing Grasberg force majeure in Indonesia to create a supply squeeze analysts expect to last through summer.

headline: NEWMONT POSTS RECORD $3.1B QUARTERLY FREE CASH FLOW; EPS Smashes Estimates at $2.90 vs $2.18 Expected
snippet: Newmont (NYSE: NEM) generated $3.1 billion in free cash flow in Q1 2026 — a record for any single quarter in the company's history — with adjusted EPS of $2.90 crushing the $2.18 consensus estimate, driven by gold averaging ~$4,700/oz. The board approved an additional $6 billion share repurchase program, and the stock surged 8.5%, validating the thesis that gold miners are finally delivering the margin expansion the gold price has long promised.

headline: Gallium Crunch Opens Doors: Two Mining Stocks as Washington Races to Break China's 80% Grip on Critical Minerals
snippet: With China controlling over 80% of global gallium production, Alcoa and Teck Resources are highlighted as two miners positioned to benefit from Western critical mineral supply chain diversification programs. The piece draws an explicit parallel to the rare earth supply crunch — and what it historically meant for early-mover junior and mid-tier miners who got there ahead of the newsletter crowd.

headline: WORLD'S LARGEST LITHIUM MINE Slashes Guidance; IGO Shares Crash 14% on Greenbushes 'Systemic Issues'
snippet: Australian miner IGO Ltd. fell as much as 14% after slashing full-year output guidance at Greenbushes — the world's largest hard-rock lithium mine — to 1.38–1.43 million tonnes from 1.50–1.65 million tonnes previously, citing 'systemic issues' across safety, feed grade, recoveries, maintenance execution, and plant reliability. The downgrade is a significant supply shock for a market that had only recently begun pricing in tighter fundamentals after years of oversupply.

headline: Contango Silver & Gold Deploys $63M Exploration War Chest Across Alaska and Northern BC in 2026
snippet: Contango Silver & Gold Inc. outlined a $63 million exploration program for 2026 targeting advanced gold and critical metals projects across Alaska and Northern British Columbia, one of the larger junior exploration commitments announced this season. The program underscores how sustained high precious metals prices are finally unlocking budgets that sat dormant through years of bear market paralysis.

headline: Brazilian Rare Earths Monte Alto Hits 35.3% TREO in Drilling; Trial Mining Licence Granted for Pilot Plant
snippet: Brazilian Rare Earths (ASX: BRE / OTCQX: BRELY) published its March 2026 quarterly activities report highlighting ultra-high-grade drill intercepts up to 35.3% TREO at Monte Alto, with NdPr grades up to 59,645 ppm and ore-sorting upgrades to 27% TREO in a single pass. Brazil's National Mining Agency (ANM) has issued a Trial Mining Licence for up to 2,000 tpa, with pilot plant commissioning at Camaçari targeted for Q3 2026.

headline: Uranium Producers Fast-Track ISR Projects as Spot Holds Above $80/lb — First New U.S. Mines in Over a Decade
snippet: Uranium spot prices have held above $80/lb throughout 2026, currently near $86.90/lb — well above the sub-$65 range that defined most of 2025 — incentivizing producers globally to fast-track in-situ recovery operations to production. New ISR mines coming online in Wyoming and Texas mark the most significant expansion of domestic U.S. uranium production capacity in over a decade, as utilities face a tightening long-term contract market.

headline: S&P GLOBAL: Copper and Gold Supply Constraints Converging to Deliver Exceptional Cash Flow Year for Diversified Miners
snippet: S&P Global Market Intelligence's April 2026 copper and gold market outlook examines how simultaneous supply constraints — force majeures, permitting delays, and input cost inflation — are combining with $4,700+ gold and $6/lb copper to generate exceptional free cash flow margins across diversified miners. Rising energy and labour costs are identified as the key risk to margin expansion for the balance of 2026.
